overall_assessment: RR-AUTH-04 has real regression coverage for resolver, reset, and landlord-profile paths, but the test lane is not audit-clean. The public-auth risk-matrix claim is still proved only by static config inspection, stale-token invalidation after reissue is not directly exercised, and one new unit test leaks frozen time across the suite. The missing red-run/TDD provenance is documented in the TODO and is not treated as a separate blocking finding here.
recommended_path: Address the findings and rerun the test-quality audit before marking RR-AUTH-04 test-quality clean.

findings.title: Public-auth risk-matrix coverage is static-config only
findings.rationale: At tests/Feature/Security/ApiSecurityHardeningMiddlewareTest.php:329-353, the new audit only reads config('api_security.risk_matrix') and compares literal pattern, method, and limit values. The protected endpoints live separately at routes/api/public_tenant_maybe_api_v1.php:93-107, but no RR-AUTH-04 test drives those real routes through the security middleware and proves the expected domain/level behavior. A route rename or regex drift can therefore detach an auth endpoint from its abuse controls while this new test and the string-based architecture guardrail still stay green.
findings.suggested_action: Promote this lane to route-behavior evidence by proving the actual tenant-public auth endpoints are classified and enforced as intended, not just that matching config rows exist.

findings.title: Reset-token reissue behavior is asserted indirectly, not by invalidating the older token
findings.rationale: tests/Api/v1/Admin/ApiV1AdminProfileTest.php:32-40 proves that two issued tokens differ, and tests/Unit/Application/Auth/PasswordResetTokenServiceTest.php:54-94 proves that a consumed token cannot be reused, but no test actually attempts to use the first token after a second issue. RR-AUTH-04's contract says reissuing a token deletes the prior token for that user; today the suite would still pass if older tokens remained usable alongside the newest hash.
findings.suggested_action: Add slice-level evidence that reissuing a reset token invalidates the earlier token and leaves only the latest token usable.

findings.title: PasswordResetTokenServiceTest freezes global time without clearing it
findings.rationale: tests/Unit/Application/Auth/PasswordResetTokenServiceTest.php:33 calls Carbon::setTestNow(...), but the class and its supporting refresh trait do not clear that global clock afterward. Because Carbon test time is process-global, later tests can inherit the frozen timestamp, masking expiry regressions or producing order-dependent failures unrelated to RR-AUTH-04 behavior.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the frozen-clock scope local to the assertion and ensure the suite clears test time after the test completes.
